{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_2b702340dd5e4e8fae7dd0e4","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# ARMOR\n2\t\n3\t**Authenticated Range-readable Managed Object Repository**\n4\t\n5\tARMOR is an S3-compatible proxy server that transparently encrypts data before storing it in [Backblaze B2](https://www.backblaze.com/cloud-storage) and serves downloads through Cloudflare for zero-egress cost. Any S3-compatible client — boto3, AWS CLI, DuckDB, rclone — works without modification.\n6\t\n7\t- **Zero-knowledge encryption** — data is encrypted before it leaves ARMOR; B2 only ever stores ciphertext\n8\t- **Zero egress fees** — downloads route through Cloudflare via the Bandwidth Alliance\n9\t- **Seekable encryption** — AES-256-CTR with 64KB blocks enables byte-range reads without decrypting the whole file\n10\t- **DuckDB-compatible** — query encrypted Parquet files with column pruning and predicate pushdown intact\n11\t- **Multi-key routing** — different master keys for different path prefixes; automatic key selection per object\n12\t\n13\t## Quick Start\n14\t\n15\t### Docker\n16\t\n17\t```bash\n18\tdocker run -d \\\n19\t  -p 9000:9000 \\\n20\t  -p 9001:9001 \\\n21\t  -e ARMOR_B2_REGION=us-east-005 \\\n22\t  -e ARMOR_B2_ACCESS_KEY_ID=your-key-id \\\n23\t  -e ARMOR_B2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=your-key-secret \\\n24\t  -e ARMOR_BUCKET=your-bucket \\\n25\t  -e ARMOR_CF_DOMAIN=your-cf-domain.example.com \\\n26\t  -e ARMOR_MEK=$(openssl rand -hex 32) \\\n27\t  -e ARMOR_AUTH_ACCESS_KEY=my-access-key \\\n28\t  -e ARMOR_AUTH_SECRET_KEY=my-secret-key \\\n29\t  ronaldraygun/armor:0.1.43\n30\t```\n31\t\n32\t> **Note:** The ARMOR CI pipeline auto-bumps the VERSION file on every build and publishes the container image as `ronaldraygun/armor:<version>` to Docker Hub. Always pin to a specific version tag in production deployments. Use the latest published tag from [Docker Hub](https://hub.docker.com/r/ronaldraygun/armor/tags).\n33\t\n34\t### Client Configuration\n35\t\n36\tPoint any S3-compatible tool at ARMOR's listen address:\n37\t\n38\t```bash\n39\t# AWS CLI\n40\taws --endpoint-url http://localhost:9000 s3 cp file.txt s3://bucket/key\n41\t\n42\t# boto3 (Python)\n43\timport boto3\n44\ts3 = boto3.client('s3',\n45\t    endpoint_url='http://localhost:9000',\n46\t    aws_access_key_id='my-access-key',\n47\t    aws_secret_access_key='my-secret-key')\n48\ts3.upload_file('local.txt', 'bucket', 'key')\n49\t\n50\t# DuckDB\n51\tINSTALL httpfs;\n52\tLOAD httpfs;\n53\tSET s3_endpoint='localhost:9000';\n54\tSET s3_access_key_id='my-access-key';\n55\tSET s3_secret_access_key='my-secret-key';\n56\tSELECT * FROM read_parquet('s3://bucket/data.parquet');\n57\t```\n58\t\n59\t## Cost Model\n60\t\n61\t| Component | Cost |\n62\t|-----------|------|\n63\t| Storage | ~$6–7/TB/month |\n64\t| Egress (via Cloudflare Bandwidth Alliance) | $0 |\n65\t| B2 API calls | $0 |\n66\t| Cloudflare (free plan) | $0 |\n67\t| **Total** | **~$6–7/TB/month** |\n68\t\n69\t## Architecture\n70\t\n71\t### Upload Path (direct to B2 — ingress is free)\n72\t\n73\t```\n74\t┌──────────┐     ┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────┐\n75\t│  Client   │────▶│    ARMOR     │────▶│    B2    │\n76\t│           │     │  encrypt +   │     │          │\n77\t│           │     │  upload      │     │          │\n78\t└──────────┘     └──────────────┘     └──────────┘\n79\t```\n80\t\n81\t### Download Path (through Cloudflare — egress is free)\n82\t\n83\t```\n84\t┌──────────┐     ┌────────────┐     ┌────────────┐     ┌──────────┐\n85\t│  Client   │◀───│ Cloudflare │◀───│  Cloudflare │◀───│    B2    │\n86\t│  ARMOR    │    │   Edge     │    │  PNI Link   │    │          │\n87\t│  decrypt  │    │  (cache)   │    │  (free)     │    │          │\n88\t└──────────┘     └────────────┘     └────────────┘     └──────────┘\n89\t```\n90\t\n91\t### DuckDB Query Path (seekable decryption)\n92\t\n93\tDuckDB issues byte-range GET requests for specific row groups and columns. ARMOR decrypts only the requested 64KB blocks, so column pruning and predicate pushdown remain effective:\n94\t\n95\t```\n96\tDuckDB                          ARMOR                       Cloudflare → B2\n97\t  │                                │                              │\n98\t  ├─ read footer (last 8 bytes) ──▶├─ Range GET (encrypted) ────▶│\n99\t  │◀── decrypted footer ──────────┤◀── ciphertext ──────────────┤\n100\t  │                                │                              │\n101\t  ├─ read col_a, row group 3 ────▶├─ Range GET (3 blocks) ─────▶│\n102\t  │◀── decrypted column chunk ────┤◀── ciphertext ──────────────┤\n103\t  │                                │                              │\n104\t  └─ result set                    └                              └\n105\t```\n106\t\n107\t## Encryption Design\n108\t\n109\t```\n110\tMaster Key (MEK)\n111\t │  stored locally, never uploaded\n112\t │\n113\t └─▶ wraps ──▶ Data Encryption Key (DEK)\n114\t                │  random per-file, wrapped copy in B2 metadata\n115\t                │\n116\t                └─▶ encrypts ──▶ File Data\n117\t                                   AES-256-CTR, 64KB blocks\n118\t                                   per-block HMAC-SHA256\n119\t                                   seekable random access\n120\t```\n121\t\n122\tKey rotation re-wraps DEKs without re-uploading file data — a metadata-only operation.\n123\t\n124\t## Security Model\n125\t\n126\t| Threat | Mitigation |\n127\t|--------|-----------|\n128\t| B2 breach | Client-side encryption — B2 only stores opaque blobs |\n129\t| Cloudflare inspection | Client-side encryption — Cloudflare only caches opaque blobs |\n130\t| Man-in-the-middle | TLS everywhere + client-side encryption |\n131\t| Key compromise | Envelope encryption — per-file DEKs limit blast radius; rotation re-wraps without re-uploading |\n132\t| Data corruption | SHA-256 integrity hash + per-block HMACs |\n133\t| Unauthorized access | Private bucket + Cloudflare Worker auth + scoped application keys |\n134\t\n135\t## Configuration\n136\t\n137\tARMOR is configured via environment variables:\n138\t\n139\t| Variable | Required | Default | Description |\n140\t|----------|----------|---------|-------------|\n141\t| `ARMOR_LISTEN` | No | `0.0.0.0:9000` | S3 API listen address |\n142\t| `ARMOR_ADMIN_LISTEN` | No | `127.0.0.1:9001` | Admin API (key rotation, canary, audit) |\n143\t| `ARMOR_B2_REGION` | Yes | — | B2 region (e.g., `us-east-005`) |\n144\t| `ARMOR_B2_ACCESS_KEY_ID` | Yes | — | B2 application key ID |\n145\t| `ARMOR_B2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY` | Yes | — | B2 application key |\n146\t| `ARMOR_BUCKET` | Yes | — | B2 bucket name |\n147\t| `ARMOR_CF_DOMAIN` | Yes | — | Cloudflare domain CNAME'd to B2 |\n148\t| `ARMOR_MEK` | Yes | — | Master encryption key (hex, 32 bytes) |\n149\t| `ARMOR_AUTH_ACCESS_KEY` | No | (random) | Client access key |\n150\t| `ARMOR_AUTH_SECRET_KEY` | No | (random) | Client secret key |\n151\t| `ARMOR_BLOCK_SIZE` | No | `65536` | Encryption block size (bytes) |\n152\t| `ARMOR_WRITER_ID` | No | (hostname) | Provenance chain writer ID |\n153\t| `ARMOR_READYZ_CACHE_TTL` | No | `30` | Seconds to cache backend connectivity in `/readyz` |\n154\t| `ARMOR_DASHBOARD_USER` | No | — | Dashboard HTTP Basic Auth username |\n155\t| `ARMOR_DASHBOARD_PASS` | No | — | Dashboard HTTP Basic Auth password |\n156\t| `ARMOR_DASHBOARD_TOKEN` | No | — | Dashboard Bearer token |\n157\t\n158\t### Multi-Key Routing\n159\t\n160\tRoute different path prefixes to different master keys:\n161\t\n162\t```bash\n163\tARMOR_MEK=<hex>                           # default key\n164\tARMOR_MEK_SENSITIVE=<hex>                 # named key\n165\tARMOR_MEK_ARCHIVE=<hex>                   # named key\n166\tARMOR_KEY_ROUTES=\"data/pii/*=sensitive,archive/*=archive,*=default\"\n167\t```\n168\t\n169\t### Multi-Credential Configuration\n170\t\n171\t```bash\n172\tARMOR_AUTH_READONLY_ACCESS_KEY=reader-key\n173\tARMOR_AUTH_READONLY_SECRET_KEY=reader-secret\n174\tARMOR_AUTH_READONLY_ACL=\"mybucket:readonly/*\"\n175\t\n176\tARMOR_AUTH_WRITER_ACCESS_KEY=writer-key\n177\tARMOR_AUTH_WRITER_SECRET_KEY=writer-secret\n178\tARMOR_AUTH_WRITER_ACL=\"mybucket:*,otherbucket:uploads/*\"\n179\t```\n180\t\n181\t## S3 API Coverage\n182\t\n183\t### Transforming Operations (encryption/decryption applied)\n184\t\n185\t| Operation | Support |\n186\t|-----------|---------|\n187\t| PutObject | Full (streaming for large files) |\n188\t| GetObject | Full (range reads) |\n189\t| HeadObject | Full (plaintext size, conditionals) |\n190\t| CopyObject | Full (DEK re-wrapping, cross-bucket) |\n191\t| CreateMultipartUpload | Full |\n192\t| UploadPart | Full |\n193\t| CompleteMultipartUpload | Full |\n194\t| AbortMultipartUpload | Full |\n195\t| ListParts | Full |\n196\t| ListMultipartUploads | Full |\n197\t\n198\t### Passthrough Operations\n199\t\n200\t| Operation | Support |\n201\t|-----------|---------|\n202\t| ListObjectsV2 | Full (size correction, `.armor/` filter) |\n203\t| DeleteObject | Full |\n204\t| DeleteObjects | Full |\n205\t| ListBuckets | Full |\n206\t| CreateBucket / DeleteBucket / HeadBucket | Full |\n207\t| Lifecycle configuration | Full |\n208\t| Object Lock / Retention / Legal Hold | Full |\n209\t\n210\t**Reserved Namespace: `.armor/`**\n211\t\n212\tThe `.armor/` prefix is reserved for ARMOR internal use. Client operations targeting keys with this prefix return `403 AccessDenied`. This protects:\n213\t\n214\t- `.armor/chain/<writer>/*` — Tamper-evident provenance chain entries\n215\t- `.armor/chain-head/<writer>` — Provenance chain head pointers\n216\t- `.armor/manifest/<writer>/*` — Manifest delta files (IV + wrapped DEK entries)\n217\t- `.armor/hmac/<sha256>` — Multipart upload HMAC sidecars\n218\t- `.armor/rotation-state.json` — In-progress key rotation state\n219\t- `.armor/multipart/*.state` — Crash recovery state for multipart uploads\n220\t- `.armor/canary/*` — Health check canary objects\n221\t\n222\tInternal ARMOR components (provenance recorder, manifest persistence, canary, key rotation, multipart state manager) access these keys directly through the backend layer, bypassing the S3 handler guard.\n223\t\n224\t## Web Dashboard\n225\t\n226\tA web dashboard for bucket browsing, encryption status, and metrics is available on the admin port (default `127.0.0.1:9001`):\n227\t\n228\t```bash\n229\topen http://localhost:9001/dashboard\n230\t```\n231\t\n232\tFeatures:\n233\t- Bucket browsing with prefix-based navigation\n234\t- Encryption status badges per object (key name, ARMOR vs. unencrypted)\n235\t- Metadata cache hit rates\n236\t- Real-time metrics: requests, bytes transferred, uptime, canary status\n237\t\n238\tSee [docs/dashboard.md](docs/dashboard.md) for full documentation.\n239\t\n240\t## Admin API\n241\t\n242\tKey management and monitoring endpoints on the admin listener (`127.0.0.1:9001`):\n243\t\n244\t| Endpoint | Method | Description |\n245\t|----------|--------|-------------|\n246\t| `/healthz` | GET | Liveness check |\n247\t| `/readyz` | GET | Readiness check (verifies B2 connectivity) |\n248\t| `/metrics` | GET | Prometheus metrics |\n249\t| `/admin/key/verify` | GET | Verify MEK can decrypt the canary object |\n250\t| `/admin/key/rotate` | POST | Rotate MEK — re-wraps all DEKs, no file re-upload |\n251\t| `/admin/key/export` | GET | Export current MEK (`?confirm=yes`) |\n252\t| `/admin/audit` | GET | Walk provenance chains, verify integrity |\n253\t| `/admin/presign` | POST | Generate pre-signed share URL |\n254\t| `/armor/canary` | GET | Canary integrity status |\n255\t| `/dashboard` | GET | Web dashboard |\n256\t\n257\t## Repository Structure\n258\t\n259\t```\n260\tARMOR/\n261\t├── cmd/armor/main.go          # Entrypoint\n262\t├── internal/\n263\t│   ├── server/                # S3 API handlers, auth\n264\t│   ├── crypto/                # Encryption, decryption, envelope key management\n265\t│   ├── backend/               # B2 S3 client, Cloudflare download routing\n266\t│   ├── canary/                # Self-healing integrity monitor\n267\t│   ├── config/                # Cloudflare\n288\t\n289\t## Disaster Recovery / Offline Decryption\n290\t\n291\tARMOR includes a standalone CLI tool `armor-decrypt` for recovering encrypted objects without a running ARMOR server. This enables disaster recovery scenarios where you have:\n292\t\n293\t- The Master Encryption Key (MEK)\n294\t- Access to B2 (or a local copy of an encrypted object)\n295\t\n296\t### Building the Decrypt Tool\n297\t\n298\t```bash\n299\tgo build -o armor-decrypt ./cmd/armor-decrypt\n300\t```\n301\t\n302\t### Usage\n303\t\n304\t#### Decrypt from B2\n305\t\n306\t```bash\n307\t# Decrypt directly from B2 (requires B2 credentials)\n308\tarmor-decrypt \\\n309\t  -mek 0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef \\\n310\t  -input b2://my-bucket/path/to/file.encrypted \\\n311\t  -output recovered-file.txt\n312\t\n313\t# Using MEK from environment\n314\texport ARMOR_MEK=0123456789abcdef...\n315\tarmor-decrypt -input b2://my-bucket/file -output recovered.txt\n316\t\n317\t# With verbose output\n318\tarmor-decrypt -mek HEX -input b2://bucket/file -v - output recovered.txt\n319\t```\n320\t\n321\t#### Decrypt from Local File\n322\t\n323\tFor local files, you need the wrapped DEK (from `x-amz-meta-armor-wrapped-dek` metadata):\n324\t\n325\t```bash\n326\tarmor-decrypt \\\n327\t  -mek 0123456789abcdef... \\\n328\t  -input /path/to/encrypted.bin \\\n329\t  -wrapped-dek WWF...base64... \\\n330\t  -output plaintext.bin\n331\t```\n332\t\n333\t### Key Requirements\n334\t\n335\t- **MEK (Master Encryption Key)**: 32-byte hex string\n336\t- **For B2**: `ARMOR_B2_REGION`, `ARMOR_B2_ENDPOINT`, `ARMOR_B2_ACCESS_KEY_ID`, `ARMOR_B2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`\n337\t- **For local files**: Wrapped DEK (base64, from object metadata)\n338\t\n339\t### Multi-Key Support\n340\t\n341\tIf your ARMOR deployment uses named keys (via `ARMOR_KEY_ROUTES`), specify the key ID:\n342\t\n343\t```bash\n344\tarmor-decrypt \\\n345\t  -mek <hex-for-specific-key> \\\n346\t  -input b2://bucket/file \\\n347\t  -key-id sensitive \\\n348\t  -output recovered.txt\n349\t```\n350\t\n351\tThe key ID comes from the `x-amz-meta-armor-key-id` metadata header.\n352\t\n353\t### Verification\n354\t\n355\tThe decrypt tool automatically:\n356\t\n357\t- Verifies per-block HMAC-SHA256 integrity\n358\t- Validates the plaintext SHA-256 checksum\n359\t- Detects corrupted blocks or wrong MEK\n360\t\n361\tExit codes:\n362\t- `0`: Success\n363\t- `1`: Decryption failed (wrong MEK, corrupted data, HMAC mismatch)\n364\t\n365\t### Example Workflow\n366\t\n367\t```bash\n368\t# 1. List objects to find the target\n369\taws s3 ls --endpoint-url http://localhost:9000 s3://bucket/\n370\t\n371\t# 2. Get metadata to see key requirements\n372\taws s3api head-object --endpoint-url http://localhost:9000 \\\n373\t  --bucket bucket --key file\n374\t\n375\t# 3. Decrypt with the correct MEK\n376\tarmor-decrypt -mek $ARMOR_MEK -input b2://bucket/file -output recovered\n377\t\n378\t# 4. Verify the recovered file\n379\tsha256sum recovered  # Should match x-amz-meta-armor-plaintext-sha256\n380\t```\n381\t\n382\t## License\n383\t\n384\tMIT\n385\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8404cace-c4cf-44a1-a04e-cbf316f8f6e3","uuid":"d3104f25-d091-4887-b156-f902eb9d86f8","timestamp":"2026-07-09T10:26:30.333Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/README.md","content":"# ARMOR\n\n**Authenticated Range-readable Managed Object Repository**\n\nARMOR is an S3-compatible proxy server that transparently encrypts data before storing it in [Backblaze B2](https://www.backblaze.com/cloud-storage) and serves downloads through Cloudflare for zero-egress cost. Any S3-compatible client — boto3, AWS CLI, DuckDB, rclone — works without modification.\n\n- **Zero-knowledge encryption** — data is encrypted before it leaves ARMOR; B2 only ever stores ciphertext\n- **Zero egress fees** — downloads route through Cloudflare via the Bandwidth Alliance\n- **Seekable encryption** — AES-256-CTR with 64KB blocks enables byte-range reads without decrypting the whole file\n- **DuckDB-compatible** — query encrypted Parquet files with column pruning and predicate pushdown intact\n- **Multi-key routing** — different master keys for different path prefixes; automatic key selection per object\n\n## Quick Start\n\n### Docker\n\n```bash\ndocker run -d \\\n  -p 9000:9000 \\\n  -p 9001:9001 \\\n  -e ARMOR_B2_REGION=us-east-005 \\\n  -e ARMOR_B2_ACCESS_KEY_ID=your-key-id \\\n  -e ARMOR_B2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=your-key-secret \\\n  -e ARMOR_BUCKET=your-bucket \\\n  -e ARMOR_CF_DOMAIN=your-cf-domain.example.com \\\n  -e ARMOR_MEK=$(openssl rand -hex 32) \\\n  -e ARMOR_AUTH_ACCESS_KEY=my-access-key \\\n  -e ARMOR_AUTH_SECRET_KEY=my-secret-key \\\n  ronaldraygun/armor:0.1.43\n```\n\n> **Note:** The ARMOR CI pipeline auto-bumps the VERSION file on every build and publishes the container image as `ronaldraygun/armor:<version>` to Docker Hub. Always pin to a specific version tag in production deployments. Use the latest published tag from [Docker Hub](https://hub.docker.com/r/ronaldraygun/armor/tags).\n\n### Client Configuration\n\nPoint any S3-compatible tool at ARMOR's listen address:\n\n```bash\n# AWS CLI\naws --endpoint-url http://localhost:9000 s3 cp file.txt s3://bucket/key\n\n# boto3 (Python)\nimport boto3\ns3 = boto3.client('s3',\n    endpoint_url='http://localhost:9000',\n    aws_access_key_id='my-access-key',\n    aws_secret_access_key='my-secret-key')\ns3.upload_file('local.txt', 'bucket', 'key')\n\n# DuckDB\nINSTALL httpfs;\nLOAD httpfs;\nSET s3_endpoint='localhost:9000';\nSET s3_access_key_id='my-access-key';\nSET s3_secret_access_key='my-secret-key';\nSELECT * FROM read_parquet('s3://bucket/data.parquet');\n```\n\n## Cost Model\n\n| Component | Cost |\n|-----------|------|\n| Storage | ~$6–7/TB/month |\n| Egress (via Cloudflare Bandwidth Alliance) | $0 |\n| B2 API calls | $0 |\n| Cloudflare (free plan) | $0 |\n| **Total** | **~$6–7/TB/month** |\n\n## Architecture\n\n### Upload Path (direct to B2 — ingress is free)\n\n```\n┌──────────┐     ┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────┐\n│  Client   │────▶│    ARMOR     │────▶│    B2    │\n│           │     │  encrypt +   │     │          │\n│           │     │  upload      │     │          │\n└──────────┘     └──────────────┘     └──────────┘\n```\n\n### Download Path (through Cloudflare — egress is free)\n\n```\n┌──────────┐     ┌────────────┐     ┌────────────┐     ┌──────────┐\n│  Client   │◀───│ Cloudflare │◀───│  Cloudflare │◀───│    B2    │\n│  ARMOR    │    │   Edge     │    │  PNI Link   │    │          │\n│  decrypt  │    │  (cache)   │    │  (free)     │    │          │\n└──────────┘     └────────────┘     └────────────┘     └──────────┘\n```\n\n### DuckDB Query Path (seekable decryption)\n\nDuckDB issues byte-range GET requests for specific row groups and columns. ARMOR decrypts only the requested 64KB blocks, so column pruning and predicate pushdown remain effective:\n\n```\nDuckDB                          ARMOR                       Cloudflare → B2\n  │                                │                              │\n  ├─ read footer (last 8 bytes) ──▶├─ Range GET (encrypted) ────▶│\n  │◀── decrypted footer ──────────┤◀── ciphertext ──────────────┤\n  │                                │                              │\n  ├─ read col_a, row group 3 ────▶├─ Range GET (3 blocks) ─────▶│\n  │◀── decrypted column chunk ────┤◀── ciphertext ──────────────┤\n  │                                │                              │\n  └─ result set                    └                              └\n```\n\n## Encryption Design\n\n```\nMaster Key (MEK)\n │  stored locally, never uploaded\n │\n └─▶ wraps ──▶ Data Encryption Key (DEK)\n                │  random per-file, wrapped copy in B2 metadata\n                │\n                └─▶ encrypts ──▶ File Data\n                                   AES-256-CTR, 64KB blocks\n                                   per-block HMAC-SHA256\n                                   seekable random access\n```\n\nKey rotation re-wraps DEKs without re-uploading file data — a metadata-only operation.\n\n## Security Model\n\n| Threat | Mitigation |\n|--------|-----------|\n| B2 breach | Client-side encryption — B2 only stores opaque blobs |\n| Cloudflare inspection | Client-side encryption — Cloudflare only caches opaque blobs |\n| Man-in-the-middle | TLS everywhere + client-side encryption |\n| Key compromise | Envelope encryption — per-file DEKs limit blast radius; rotation re-wraps without re-uploading |\n| Data corruption | SHA-256 integrity hash + per-block HMACs |\n| Unauthorized access | Private bucket + Cloudflare Worker auth + scoped application keys |\n\n## Configuration\n\nARMOR is configured via environment variables:\n\n| Variable | Required | Default | Description |\n|----------|----------|---------|-------------|\n| `ARMOR_LISTEN` | No | `0.0.0.0:9000` | S3 API listen address |\n| `ARMOR_ADMIN_LISTEN` | No | `127.0.0.1:9001` | Admin API (key rotation, canary, audit) |\n| `ARMOR_B2_REGION` | Yes | — | B2 region (e.g., `us-east-005`) |\n| `ARMOR_B2_ACCESS_KEY_ID` | Yes | — | B2 application key ID |\n| `ARMOR_B2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY` | Yes | — | B2 application key |\n| `ARMOR_BUCKET` | Yes | — | B2 bucket name |\n| `ARMOR_CF_DOMAIN` | Yes | — | Cloudflare domain CNAME'd to B2 |\n| `ARMOR_MEK` | Yes | — | Master encryption key (hex, 32 bytes) |\n| `ARMOR_AUTH_ACCESS_KEY` | No | (random) | Client access key |\n| `ARMOR_AUTH_SECRET_KEY` | No | (random) | Client secret key |\n| `ARMOR_BLOCK_SIZE` | No | `65536` | Encryption block size (bytes) |\n| `ARMOR_WRITER_ID` | No | (hostname) | Provenance chain writer ID |\n| `ARMOR_READYZ_CACHE_TTL` | No | `30` | Seconds to cache backend connectivity in `/readyz` |\n| `ARMOR_DASHBOARD_USER` | No | — | Dashboard HTTP Basic Auth username |\n| `ARMOR_DASHBOARD_PASS` | No | — | Dashboard HTTP Basic Auth password |\n| `ARMOR_DASHBOARD_TOKEN` | No | — | Dashboard Bearer token |\n\n### Multi-Key Routing\n\nRoute different path prefixes to different master keys:\n\n```bash\nARMOR_MEK=<hex>                           # default key\nARMOR_MEK_SENSITIVE=<hex>                 # named key\nARMOR_MEK_ARCHIVE=<hex>                   # named key\nARMOR_KEY_ROUTES=\"data/pii/*=sensitive,archive/*=archive,*=default\"\n```\n\n### Multi-Credential Configuration\n\n```bash\nARMOR_AUTH_READONLY_ACCESS_KEY=reader-key\nARMOR_AUTH_READONLY_SECRET_KEY=reader-secret\nARMOR_AUTH_READONLY_ACL=\"mybucket:readonly/*\"\n\nARMOR_AUTH_WRITER_ACCESS_KEY=writer-key\nARMOR_AUTH_WRITER_SECRET_KEY=writer-secret\nARMOR_AUTH_WRITER_ACL=\"mybucket:*,otherbucket:uploads/*\"\n```\n\n## S3 API Coverage\n\n### Transforming Operations (encryption/decryption applied)\n\n| Operation | Support |\n|-----------|---------|\n| PutObject | Full (streaming for large files) |\n| GetObject | Full (range reads) |\n| HeadObject | Full (plaintext size, conditionals) |\n| CopyObject | Full (DEK re-wrapping, cross-bucket) |\n| CreateMultipartUpload | Full |\n| UploadPart | Full |\n| CompleteMultipartUpload | Full |\n| AbortMultipartUpload | Full |\n| ListParts | Full |\n| ListMultipartUploads | Full |\n\n### Passthrough Operations\n\n| Operation | Support |\n|-----------|---------|\n| ListObjectsV2 | Full (size correction, `.armor/` filter) |\n| DeleteObject | Full |\n| DeleteObjects | Full |\n| ListBuckets | Full |\n| CreateBucket / DeleteBucket / HeadBucket | Full |\n| Lifecycle configuration | Full |\n| Object Lock / Retention / Legal Hold | Full |\n\n**Reserved Namespace: `.armor/`**\n\nThe `.armor/` prefix is reserved for ARMOR internal use. Client operations targeting keys with this prefix return `403 AccessDenied`. This protects:\n\n- `.armor/chain/<writer>/*` — Tamper-evident provenance chain entries\n- `.armor/chain-head/<writer>` — Provenance chain head pointers\n- `.armor/manifest/<writer>/*` — Manifest delta files (IV + wrapped DEK entries)\n- `.armor/hmac/<sha256>` — Multipart upload HMAC sidecars\n- `.armor/rotation-state.json` — In-progress key rotation state\n- `.armor/multipart/*.state` — Crash recovery state for multipart uploads\n- `.armor/canary/*` — Health check canary objects\n\nInternal ARMOR components (provenance recorder, manifest persistence, canary, key rotation, multipart state manager) access these keys directly through the backend layer, bypassing the S3 handler guard.\n\n## Web Dashboard\n\nA web dashboard for bucket browsing, encryption status, and metrics is available on the admin port (default `127.0.0.1:9001`):\n\n```bash\nopen http://localhost:9001/dashboard\n```\n\nFeatures:\n- Bucket browsing with prefix-based navigation\n- Encryption status badges per object (key name, ARMOR vs. unencrypted)\n- Metadata cache hit rates\n- Real-time metrics: requests, bytes transferred, uptime, canary status\n\nSee [docs/dashboard.md](docs/dashboard.md) for full documentation.\n\n## Admin API\n\nKey management and monitoring endpoints on the admin listener (`127.0.0.1:9001`):\n\n| Endpoint | Method | Description |\n|----------|--------|-------------|\n| `/healthz` | GET | Liveness check |\n| `/readyz` | GET | Readiness check (verifies B2 connectivity) |\n| `/metrics` | GET | Prometheus metrics |\n| `/admin/key/verify` | GET | Verify MEK can decrypt the canary object |\n| `/admin/key/rotate` | POST | Rotate MEK — re-wraps all DEKs, no file re-upload |\n| `/admin/key/export` | GET | Export current MEK (`?confirm=yes`) |\n| `/admin/audit` | GET | Walk provenance chains, verify integrity |\n| `/admin/presign` | POST | Generate pre-signed share URL |\n| `/armor/canary` | GET | Canary integrity status |\n| `/dashboard` | GET | Web dashboard |\n\n## Repository Structure\n\n```\nARMOR/\n├── cmd/armor/main.go          # Entrypoint\n├── internal/\n│   ├── server/                # S3 API handlers, auth\n│   ├── crypto/                # Encryption, decryption, envelope key management\n│   ├── backend/               # B2 S3 client, Cloudflare download routing\n│   ├── canary/                # Self-healing integrity monitor\n│   ├── config/                # Configuration loading (env vars)\n│   ├── keymanager/            # Multi-key routing\n│   ├── dashboard/             # Web dashboard UI and metrics\n│   ├── presign/               # Pre-signed URL generation\n│   ├── provenance/            # Cryptographic audit chain\n│   ├── logging/               # Structured JSON logging\n│   └── metrics/               # Prometheus metrics\n├── deploy/kubernetes/         # Kubernetes manifests\n├── tests/integration/         # Integration tests (requires real B2 + Cloudflare)\n└── docs/\n    ├── dashboard.md\n    ├── cloudflare-setup.md    # DNS configuration for zero-egress downloads\n    └── research/\n```\n\n## Documentation\n\n- [Disaster Recovery](docs/disaster-recovery.md) — MEK backup/escrow, restore drills, rotation failure recovery\n- [Web Dashboard](docs/dashboard.md) — Bucket browsing, encryption status, cache statistics\n- [Cloudflare Setup](docs/cloudflare-setup.md) — DNS configuration for zero-egress B2 downloads\n- [Integration Tests](tests/integration/README.md) — Testing against real B2 + Cloudflare\n\n## Disaster Recovery / Offline Decryption\n\nARMOR includes a standalone CLI tool `armor-decrypt` for recovering encrypted objects without a running ARMOR server. This enables disaster recovery scenarios where you have:\n\n- The Master Encryption Key (MEK)\n- Access to B2 (or a local copy of an encrypted object)\n\n### Building the Decrypt Tool\n\n```bash\ngo build -o armor-decrypt ./cmd/armor-decrypt\n```\n\n### Usage\n\n#### Decrypt from B2\n\n```bash\n# Decrypt directly from B2 (requires B2 credentials)\narmor-decrypt \\\n  -mek 0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef \\\n  -input b2://my-bucket/path/to/file.encrypted \\\n  -output recovered-file.txt\n\n# Using MEK from environment\nexport ARMOR_MEK=0123456789abcdef...\narmor-decrypt -input b2://my-bucket/file -output recovered.txt\n\n# With verbose output\narmor-decrypt -mek HEX -input b2://bucket/file -v - output recovered.txt\n```\n\n#### Decrypt from Local File\n\nFor local files, you need the wrapped DEK (from `x-amz-meta-armor-wrapped-dek` metadata):\n\n```bash\narmor-decrypt \\\n  -mek 0123456789abcdef... \\\n  -input /path/to/encrypted.bin \\\n  -wrapped-dek WWF...base64... \\\n  -output plaintext.bin\n```\n\n### Key Requirements\n\n- **MEK (Master Encryption Key)**: 32-byte hex string\n- **For B2**: `ARMOR_B2_REGION`, `ARMOR_B2_ENDPOINT`, `ARMOR_B2_ACCESS_KEY_ID`, `ARMOR_B2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`\n- **For local files**: Wrapped DEK (base64, from object metadata)\n\n### Multi-Key Support\n\nIf your ARMOR deployment uses named keys (via `ARMOR_KEY_ROUTES`), specify the key ID:\n\n```bash\narmor-decrypt \\\n  -mek <hex-for-specific-key> \\\n  -input b2://bucket/file \\\n  -key-id sensitive \\\n  -output recovered.txt\n```\n\nThe key ID comes from the `x-amz-meta-armor-key-id` metadata header.\n\n### Verification\n\nThe decrypt tool automatically:\n\n- Verifies per-block HMAC-SHA256 integrity\n- Validates the plaintext SHA-256 checksum\n- Detects corrupted blocks or wrong MEK\n\nExit codes:\n- `0`: Success\n- `1`: Decryption failed (wrong MEK, corrupted data, HMAC mismatch)\n\n### Example Workflow\n\n```bash\n# 1. List objects to find the target\naws s3 ls --endpoint-url http://localhost:9000 s3://bucket/\n\n# 2. Get metadata to see key requirements\naws s3api head-object --endpoint-url http://localhost:9000 \\\n  --bucket bucket --key file\n\n# 3. Decrypt with the correct MEK\narmor-decrypt -mek $ARMOR_MEK -input b2://bucket/file -output recovered\n\n# 4. Verify the recovered file\nsha256sum recovered  # Should match x-amz-meta-armor-plaintext-sha256\n```\n\n## License\n\nMIT\n","numLines":385,"startLine":1,"totalLines":385}}}

This document provides the complete command structure for debugging Pluck operations.\n10\t\n11\t## Base Command Structure\n12\t\n13\t### Minimal Command\n14\t```bash\n15\tneedle run -w <workspace> -c <count>\n16\t```\n17\t\n18\t**Parameters:**\n19\t- `-w <workspace>`: Path to the workspace directory (default: `/home/coding/ARMOR`)\n20\t- `-c <count>`: Number of operations to perform (default: `1`)\n21\t\n22\t**Example:**\n23\t```bash\n24\tneedle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1\n25\t```\n26\t\n27\t## Debug Logging Configuration\n28\t\n29\t### Environment Variable\n30\tAll debug logging is controlled via the `RUST_LOG` environment variable.\n31\t\n32\t### Debug Levels by Module\n33\t\n34\t| Module | Description | Levels |\n35\t|--------|-------------|--------|\n36\t| `needle::strand::pluck` | Core pluck strand logic | `info`, `debug`, `trace` |\n37\t| `needle::strand` | Strand framework | `info`, `debug`, `trace` |\n38\t| `needle::bead_store` | Bead data store operations | `info`, `debug`, `trace` |\n39\t| `needle::worker` | Worker lifecycle management | `info`, `debug`, `trace` |\n40\t| `needle::dispatch` | Agent dispatch logic | `info`, `debug`, `trace` |\n41\t| `needle::claim` | Bead claiming logic | `info`, `debug`, `trace` |\n42\t\n43\t## Preset Configurations\n44\t\n45\t### Level 1: Minimal\n46\t```bash\n47\texport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=info\n48\t```\n49\t**Purpose:** High-level strand operations only  \n50\t**Output:** Basic strand start/completion messages\n51\t\n52\t### Level 2: Standard (Recommended)\n53\t```bash\n54\texport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug\n55\t```\n56\t**Purpose:** Filtering decisions and statistics  \n57\t**Output:** \n58\t- Strand evaluation start\n59\t- Bead store queries with filters\n60\t- Label filtering decisions\n61\t- Candidate sorting and selection\n62\t- Split threshold checks\n63\t- Final strand results\n64\t\n65\t### Level 3: Detailed\n66\t```bash\n67\texport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace\n68\t```\n69\t**Purpose:** Complete execution details  \n70\t**Output:** Everything from Standard + detailed execution flow\n71\t\n72\t### Level 4: Comprehensive\n73\t```bash\n74\texport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug\n75\t```\n76\t**Purpose:** Pluck TRACE + supporting modules  \n77\t**Output:** Detailed pluck execution + supporting module context\n78\t\n79\t### Level 5: Full\n80\t```bash\n81\texport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug,needle::dispatch=debug,needle::claim=debug\n82\t```\n83\t**Purpose:** All NEEDLE modules at DEBUG/TRACE level  \n84\t**Output:** Complete system-wide debugging\n85\t\n86\t### Level 6: Maximum\n87\t```bash\n88\texport RUST_LOG=trace\n89\t```\n90\t**Purpose:** Everything at TRACE level  \n91\t**Output:** Extremely verbose all-module tracing  \n92\t**Warning:** Very large log files\n93\t\n94\t## Complete Execution Commands\n95\t\n96\t### Command Pattern 1: Direct Execution\n97\t```bash\n98\texport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug\n99\tneedle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1\n100\t```\n101\t\n102\t### Command Pattern 2: With Output Capture\n103\t```bash\n104\texport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug\n105\tneedle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1 2>&1 | tee pluck-debug.log\n106\t```\n107\t\n108\t### Command Pattern 3: With Timeout\n109\t```bash\n110\texport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug\n111\ttimeout 180s needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1\n112\t```\n113\t\n114\t### Command Pattern 4: Comprehensive Monitoring\n115\t```bash\n116\texport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug,needle::dispatch=debug\n117\ttimeout 180s needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1 > >(tee -a stdout.log) 2> >(tee -a stderr.log >&2)\n118\t```\n119\t\n120\t## Using the Configuration Script\n121\t\n122\t### Quick Usage\n123\t```bash\n124\tcd /home/coding/ARMOR\n125\tbash pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log standard\n126\t```\n127\t\n128\t### Available Modes\n129\t```bash\n130\tbash pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log minimal       # INFO level\n131\tbash pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log standard      # DEBUG level (recommended)\n132\tbash pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log detailed      # TRACE level\n133\tbash pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log comprehensive # Multi-module DEBUG/TRACE\n134\tbash pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log full          # All NEEDLE modules\n135\tbash pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log maximum       # Everything at TRACE\n136\t```\n137\t\n138\t## Expected Debug Output\n139\t\n140\t### When Logging is Enabled\n141\t- ✅ **Strand evaluation start** - Shows exclude_labels configuration and split_threshold\n142\t- ✅ **Bead store queries** - Filter parameters and candidate count\n143\t- ✅ **Label filtering** - Excluded beads and reasons\n144\t- ✅ **Status/assignee filtering** - Remaining candidates\n145\t- ✅ **Candidate sorting** - Priority order and first candidate\n146\t- ✅ **Split threshold checks** - Failure count analysis\n147\t- ✅ **Final results** - NoWork / BeadFound / Split\n148\t\n149\t## Log Analysis Commands\n150\t\n151\t### View Pluck Events\n152\t```bash\n153\tgrep -i \"pluck\" pluck-debug.log\n154\tgrep -i \"filter\" pluck-debug.log\n155\tgrep -i \"exclude\" pluck-debug.log\n156\tgrep -i \"candidate\" pluck-debug.log\n157\tgrep -i \"split\" pluck-debug.log\n158\t```\n159\t\n160\t### Count Events\n161\t```bash\n162\tgrep -c \"Pluck strand evaluation starting\" pluck-debug.log\n163\tgrep -c \"result=BeadFound\" Pluck Debug Command Structure - Complete Reference\n\n**Bead:** bf-1r7s  \n**Status:** ✅ Complete and Verified  \n**Last Updated:** 2026-07-09\n\n## Overview\n\nPluck is NEEDLE's bead selection strand, responsible for evaluating and selecting beads for agent execution. This document provides the complete command structure for debugging Pluck operations.\n\n## Base Command Structure\n\n### Minimal Command\n```bash\nneedle run -w <workspace> -c <count>\n```\n\n**Parameters:**\n- `-w <workspace>`: Path to the workspace directory (default: `/home/coding/ARMOR`)\n- `-c <count>`: Number of operations to perform (default: `1`)\n\n**Example:**\n```bash\nneedle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1\n```\n\n## Debug Logging Configuration\n\n### Environment Variable\nAll debug logging is controlled via the `RUST_LOG` environment variable.\n\n### Debug Levels by Module\n\n| Module | Description | Levels |\n|--------|-------------|--------|\n| `needle::strand::pluck` | Core pluck strand logic | `info`, `debug`, `trace` |\n| `needle::strand` | Strand framework | `info`, `debug`, `trace` |\n| `needle::bead_store` | Bead data store operations | `info`, `debug`, `trace` |\n| `needle::worker` | Worker lifecycle management | `info`, `debug`, `trace` |\n| `needle::dispatch` | Agent dispatch logic | `info`, `debug`, `trace` |\n| `needle::claim` | Bead claiming logic | `info`, `debug`, `trace` |\n\n## Preset Configurations\n\n### Level 1: Minimal\n```bash\nexport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=info\n```\n**Purpose:** High-level strand operations only  \n**Output:** Basic strand start/completion messages\n\n### Level 2: Standard (Recommended)\n```bash\nexport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug\n```\n**Purpose:** Filtering decisions and statistics  \n**Output:** \n- Strand evaluation start\n- Bead store queries with filters\n- Label filtering decisions\n- Candidate sorting and selection\n- Split threshold checks\n- Final strand results\n\n### Level 3: Detailed\n```bash\nexport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace\n```\n**Purpose:** Complete execution details  \n**Output:** Everything from Standard + detailed execution flow\n\n### Level 4: Comprehensive\n```bash\nexport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug\n```\n**Purpose:** Pluck TRACE + supporting modules  \n**Output:** Detailed pluck execution + supporting module context\n\n### Level 5: Full\n```bash\nexport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug,needle::dispatch=debug,needle::claim=debug\n```\n**Purpose:** All NEEDLE modules at DEBUG/TRACE level  \n**Output:** Complete system-wide debugging\n\n### Level 6: Maximum\n```bash\nexport RUST_LOG=trace\n```\n**Purpose:** Everything at TRACE level  \n**Output:** Extremely verbose all-module tracing  \n**Warning:** Very large log files\n\n## Complete Execution Commands\n\n### Command Pattern 1: Direct Execution\n```bash\nexport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug\nneedle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1\n```\n\n### Command Pattern 2: With Output Capture\n```bash\nexport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug\nneedle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1 2>&1 | tee pluck-debug.log\n```\n\n### Command Pattern 3: With Timeout\n```bash\nexport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug\ntimeout 180s needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1\n```\n\n### Command Pattern 4: Comprehensive Monitoring\n```bash\nexport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug,needle::dispatch=debug\ntimeout 180s needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1 > >(tee -a stdout.log) 2> >(tee -a stderr.log >&2)\n```\n\n## Using the Configuration Script\n\n### Quick Usage\n```bash\ncd /home/coding/ARMOR\nbash pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log standard\n```\n\n### Available Modes\n```bash\nbash pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log minimal       # INFO level\nbash pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log standard      # DEBUG level (recommended)\nbash pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log detailed      # TRACE level\nbash pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log comprehensive # Multi-module DEBUG/TRACE\nbash pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log full          # All NEEDLE modules\nbash pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log maximum       # Everything at TRACE\n```\n\n## Expected Debug Output\n\n### When Logging is Enabled\n- ✅ **Strand evaluation start** - Shows exclude_labels configuration and split_threshold\n- ✅ **Bead store queries** - Filter parameters and candidate count\n- ✅ **Label filtering** - Excluded beads and reasons\n- ✅ **Status/assignee filtering** - Remaining candidates\n- ✅ **Candidate sorting** - Priority order and first candidate\n- ✅ **Split threshold checks** - Failure count analysis\n- ✅ **Final results** - NoWork / BeadFound / Split\n\n## Log Analysis Commands\n\n### View Pluck Events\n```bash\ngrep -i \"pluck\" pluck-debug.log\ngrep -i \"filter\" pluck-debug.log\ngrep -i \"exclude\" pluck-debug.log\ngrep -i \"candidate\" pluck-debug.log\ngrep -i \"split\" pluck-debug.log\n```\n\n### Count Events\n```bash\ngrep -c \"Pluck strand evaluation starting\" pluck-debug.log\ngrep -c \"result=BeadFound\" pluck-debug.log\ngrep -c \"result=NoWork\" pluck-debug.log\ngrep -c \"result=Split\" pluck-debug.log\n```\n\n## Command Verification\n\n### Verify needle Command\n```bash\ncommand -v needle\nneedle run --help\n```\n\n### Validate Syntax\n```bash\nbash test-pluck-syntax.sh\n```\n\n### Test Configuration\n```bash\nbash pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR test-debug.log standard\n```\n\n## Complete Command Examples\n\n### Example 1: Quick Debug Check\n```bash\nexport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug\ncd /home/coding/ARMOR\nneedle run -w . -c 1\n```\n\n### Example 2: Comprehensive This document provides the complete reference for debugging Pluck strand operations using Rust's tracing infrastructure.\n11\t\n12\t## Core Debug Command Structure\n13\t\n14\t### Basic Command Pattern\n15\t\n16\t```bash\n17\tRUST_LOG=<debug_level> needle run -w <workspace> -c <concurrency>\n18\t```\n19\t\n20\t### Complete Debug Command with All Options\n21\t\n22\t```bash\n23\tRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug,needle::dispatch=debug \\\n24\tRUST_BACKTRACE=1 \\\n25\tNEEDLE run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1 2>&1 | tee pluck-debug-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log\n26\t```\n27\t\n28\t## RUST_LOG Environment Variable Configurations\n29\t\n30\t### Debug Level Hierarchy\n31\t\n32\t| Level | Purpose | When to Use |\n33\t|-------|---------|-------------|\n34\t| `error` | Critical failures only | Production monitoring |\n35\t| `warn` | Warning conditions | Production with alerts |\n36\t| `info` | High-level operations | Normal operation visibility |\n37\t| `debug` | Detailed decisions | **Recommended for debugging** |\n38\t| `trace` | Complete execution flow | Deep troubleshooting |\n39\t\n40\t### Pluck-Specific Module Targets\n41\t\n42\t| Module Target | Description |\n43\t|---------------|-------------|\n44\t| `needle::strand::pluck` | **Primary Pluck strand module** |\n45\t| `needle::strand` | All strand modules (pluck, mend, explore, weave, unravel, pulse, reflect, splice, knot) |\n46\t| `needle::bead_store` | Bead storage and query operations |\n47\t| `needle::worker` | Worker lifecycle and coordination |\n48\t| `needle::dispatch` | Task dispatch and coordination |\n49\t| `needle::claim` | Bead claiming operations |\n50\t\n51\t### Preset RUST_LOG Configurations\n52\t\n53\t#### 1. **minimal** - INFO level only\n54\t```bash\n55\texport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=info\n56\t```\n57\t**Output:** High-level strand operations only  \n58\t**Use case:** Quick health checks, basic operation verification\n59\t\n60\t#### 2. **standard** - DEBUG level (Recommended)\n61\t```bash\n62\texport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug\n63\t```\n64\t**Output:** Filtering decisions and statistics  \n65\t**Use case:** Normal debugging, understanding filtering behavior\n66\t\n67\t#### 3. **detailed** - TRACE level\n68\t```bash\n69\texport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace\n70\t```\n71\t**Output:** Complete execution details  \n72\t**Use case:** Deep troubleshooting, understanding exact flow\n73\t\n74\t#### 4. **comprehensive** - Multi-module TRACE\n75\t```bash\n76\texport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug\n77\t```\n78\t**Output:** Pluck TRACE + supporting modules DEBUG  \n79\t**Use case:** Full context debugging, understanding system interactions\n80\t\n81\t#### 5. **full** - All NEEDLE modules\n82\t```bash\n83\texport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug,needle::dispatch=debug,needle::claim=debug\n84\t```\n85\t**Output:** All critical modules at DEBUG/TRACE level  \n86\t**Use case:** Complete system debugging\n87\t\n88\t#### 6. **maximum** - Global TRACE\n89\t```bash\n90\texport RUST_LOG=trace\n91\t```\n92\t**Output:** Everything at TRACE level (very verbose)  \n93\t**Use case:** Deep system-level debugging\n94\t\n95\t## Complete NEEDLE Command Reference\n96\t\n97\t### Command Structure\n98\t\n99\t```bash\n100\tneedle [GLOBAL_OPTIONS] run [RUN_OPTIONS]\n101\t```\n102\t\n103\t### Global Options\n104\t\n105\t| Option | Short | Description | Default |\n106\t|--------|-------|-------------|---------|\n107\t| `--config <FILE>` | `-C` | Config file path | `.needle.yaml` |\n108\t| `--help` | `-h` | Show help | - |\n109\t| `--version` | `-V` | Show version | - |\n110\t\n111\t### Run Command Options\n112\t\n113\t| Option | Description | Example |\n114\t|--------|-------------|---------|\n115\t| `-w, --workspace <PATH>` | Workspace directory | `-w /home/coding/ARMOR` |\n116\t| `-c, --concurrency <NUM>` | Number of concurrent workers | `-c 1` |\n117\t| `--strands <LIST>` | Specific strands to run | `--strands pluck,mend` |\n118\t| `--one-shot` | Exit after one bead | - |\n119\t\n120\t## Pluck Strand Tracing Instrumentation\n121\t\n122\t### Tracing Span Fields\n123\t\n124\tThe Pluck strand uses `tracing::instrument` with the following fields:\n125\t\n126\t```rust\n127\t#[tracing::instrument(\n128\t    name = \"strand.pluck\",\n129\t    skip(self, store),\n130\t    fields(\n131\t        strand = \"pluck\",\n132\t        exclude_labels = ?self.exclude_labels,\n133\t        split_threshold = self.split_after_failures,\n134\t    )\n135\t)]\n136\t```\n137\t\n138\t### Debug Event Sequence\n139\t\n140\t#### 1. **Evaluation Start**\n141\t```\n142\tDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Pluck strand evaluation starting\n143\t  exclude_labels=[\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"]\n144\t  split_threshold=3\n145\t```\n146\t\n147\t#### 2. **Bead Store Query**\n148\t```\n149\tDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Querying bead store for ready candidates\n150\t  filters=Filters { \n151\t    assignee: None, \n152\t    exclude_labels: [\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"] \n153\t  }\n154\t```\n155\t\n156\t#### 3. **Query Results**\n157\t```\n158\tDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Bead store returned N candidates\n159\t  count=5\n160\t```\n161\t\n162\t#### 4. **Label Filtering**\n163\t```\n164\tDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Label filtering excluded N beads\n165\t  excluded_count=2\n166\t  remaining=3\n167\t  excluded_labels=[\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"]\n168\t```\n169\t\n170\t#### 5. **Individual Bead Exclusions**\n171\t```\n172\tDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Excluded bead due to labels\n173\t  bead_id=bf-1234\n174\t  labels=[\"deferred\", \"high-priority\"]\n175\t  excluded_reasons=[\"label:deferred\"]\n176\t```\n177\t\n178\t#### 6. **Status/Assignee Filtering**\n179\t```\n180\tDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Status/assignee filtering removed N beads\n181\t  filtered_count=1\n182\t  remaining=2\n183\t```\n184\t\n185\t#### 7. **Candidate Sorting**\n186\t```\n187\tDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Sorting N candidates by (priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)\n188\t  total=5\n189\t  first_bead_id=bf-abcd\n190\t  first_priority=1\n191\t  first_created_at=2026-07-09T12:00:00Z\n192\t```\n193\t\n194\t#### 8. **Split Trigger Check**\n195\t```\n196\tDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Checking split trigger for first candidate\n197\t  bead_id=bf-fail\n198\t  failure_count=4\n199\t  threshold=3\n200\t  split_triggered=true\n201\t```\n202\t\n203\t#### 9. **Split Decision**\n204\t```\n205\tINFO needle::strand::pluck: Split threshold reached, returning Split instruction\n206\t  bead_id=bf-fail\n207\t  failure_count=4\n208\t  threshold=3\n209\t```\n210\t\n211\t#### 10. **Final Result**\n212\t```\n213\tINFO needle::strand::pluck: Returning N candidates for processing\n214\t  count=2\n215\t  candidates=[\"bf-abcd\", \"bf-1234\"]\n216\t```\n217\t\n218\t### Error Events\n219\t\n220\t```\n221\tERROR needle::strand::pluck: Bead store query failed\n222\t  error=bf list failed\n223\t```\n224\t\n225\t## Practical Execution Examples\n226\t\n227\t### Example 1: Standard Debug Session\n228\t```bash\n229\t#!/bin/bash\n230\tWORKSPACE=\"/home/coding/ARMOR\"\n231\tLOG_FILE=\"logs/pluck-debug/pluck-debug-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log\"\n232\t\n233\tmkdir -p logs/pluck-debug\n234\t\n235\tRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug \\\n236\tNEEDLE run -w \"$WORKSPACE\" -c 1 2>&1 | tee \"$LOG_FILE\"\n237\t\n238\t# Analysis\n239\tgrep \"Pluck strand evaluation starting\" \"$LOG_FILE\"\n240\tgrep \"result=BeadFound\" \"$LOG_FILE\"\n241\tgrep \"result=NoWork\" \"$LOG_FILE\"\n242\t```\n243\t\n244\t### Example 2: Comprehensive Multi-Module Debug\n245\t```bash\n246\t#!/bin/bash\n247\tRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug \\\n248\tRUST_BACKTRACE=1 \\\n249\tcd /home/coding/NEEDLE\n250\tcargo run -- run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1 2>&1 | tee comprehensive-debug.log\n251\t```\n252\t\n253\t### Example 3: Filtered Debug with Regex\n254\t```bash\n255\t# Only show Pluck events (uses env_logger regex filtering)\n256\tRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug/[.]*pluck/ \\\n257\tNEEDLE run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1\n258\t```\n259\t\n260\t### Example 4: JSON Output for Analysis\n261\t```bash\n262\tRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug \\\n263\tNEEDLE run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1 2>&1 | jq -c 'select(.message | contains(\"pluck\"))'\n264\t```\n265\t\n266\t## Log checks\n307\tgrep \"Checking split trigger\" pluck-debug.log -A 1\n308\t```\n309\t\n310\t## Additional Environment Variables\n311\t\n312\t### RUST_BACKTRACE\n313\t```bash\n314\t# Enable backtrace on **visibility**. For comprehensive debugging:\n343\t\n344\t1. Set your desired behavior in `.needle.yaml`\n345\t2. Set appropriate `RUST_LOG` level for visibility\n346\t3. Run with command structure shown above\n347\t\n348\t## Troubleshooting\n349\t\n350\t### No Pluck Output Visible\n351\t\n352\t**Symptoms:** No pluck events in log despite setting RUST_LOG\n353\t\n354\t**Solutions:**\n355\t```bash\n356\t# Verify RUST_LOG is set\n357\techo $RUST_LOG\n358\t\n359\t# Check if Pluck strand is active\n360\tgrep \"worker booted\" pluck-debug.log | grep \"pluck\"\n361\t\n362\t# Ensure beads exist\n363\tbr list --status=open\n364\t```\n365\t\n366\t### Missing Expected Events\n367\t\n368\t**Common causes:**\n369\t1. RUST_LOG syntax error\n370\t2. Wrong module target\n371\t3. No beads in queue\n372\t4. Strand not loaded\n373\t\n374\t**Verification:**\n375\t```bash\n376\t# Test basic logging\n377\tRUST_LOG=info needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1\n378\t\n379\t# Check strand loading\n380\tRUST_LOG=needle::worker=debug needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1\n381\t```\n382\t\n383\t### Performance Impact\n384\t\n385\tHigher debug levels (especially `trace`) can impact **Recommended for debugging** |\n391\t| `trace` | Moderate | Deep troubleshooting only |\n392\t| `trace` (global) | High | Short debugging sessions only |\n393\t\n394\t## Advanced Pluck Debug Command Structure - Complete Reference\n\n**Bead:** bf-1r7s  \n**Date:** 2026-07-09  \n**Workspace:** /home/coding/ARMOR  \n**NEEDLE Version:** 0.2.11\n\n## Overview\n\nThe Pluck strand is NEEDLE's primary bead selection mechanism, handling >90% of all bead processing. This document provides the complete reference for debugging Pluck strand operations using Rust's tracing infrastructure.\n\n## Core Debug Command Structure\n\n### Basic Command Pattern\n\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=<debug_level> needle run -w <workspace> -c <concurrency>\n```\n\n### Complete Debug Command with All Options\n\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug,needle::dispatch=debug \\\nRUST_BACKTRACE=1 \\\nNEEDLE run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1 2>&1 | tee pluck-debug-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log\n```\n\n## RUST_LOG Environment Variable Configurations\n\n### Debug Level Hierarchy\n\n| Level | Purpose | When to Use |\n|-------|---------|-------------|\n| `error` | Critical failures only | Production monitoring |\n| `warn` | Warning conditions | Production with alerts |\n| `info` | High-level operations | Normal operation visibility |\n| `debug` | Detailed decisions | **Recommended for debugging** |\n| `trace` | Complete execution flow | Deep troubleshooting |\n\n### Pluck-Specific Module Targets\n\n| Module Target | Description |\n|---------------|-------------|\n| `needle::strand::pluck` | **Primary Pluck strand module** |\n| `needle::strand` | All strand modules (pluck, mend, explore, weave, unravel, pulse, reflect, splice, knot) |\n| `needle::bead_store` | Bead storage and query operations |\n| `needle::worker` | Worker lifecycle and coordination |\n| `needle::dispatch` | Task dispatch and coordination |\n| `needle::claim` | Bead claiming operations |\n\n### Preset RUST_LOG Configurations\n\n#### 1. **minimal** - INFO level only\n```bash\nexport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=info\n```\n**Output:** High-level strand operations only  \n**Use case:** Quick health checks, basic operation verification\n\n#### 2. **standard** - DEBUG level (Recommended)\n```bash\nexport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug\n```\n**Output:** Filtering decisions and statistics  \n**Use case:** Normal debugging, understanding filtering behavior\n\n#### 3. **detailed** - TRACE level\n```bash\nexport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace\n```\n**Output:** Complete execution details  \n**Use case:** Deep troubleshooting, understanding exact flow\n\n#### 4. **comprehensive** - Multi-module TRACE\n```bash\nexport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug\n```\n**Output:** Pluck TRACE + supporting modules DEBUG  \n**Use case:** Full context debugging, understanding system interactions\n\n#### 5. **full** - All NEEDLE modules\n```bash\nexport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug,needle::dispatch=debug,needle::claim=debug\n```\n**Output:** All critical modules at DEBUG/TRACE level  \n**Use case:** Complete system debugging\n\n#### 6. **maximum** - Global TRACE\n```bash\nexport RUST_LOG=trace\n```\n**Output:** Everything at TRACE level (very verbose)  \n**Use case:** Deep system-level debugging\n\n## Complete NEEDLE Command Reference\n\n### Command Structure\n\n```bash\nneedle [GLOBAL_OPTIONS] run [RUN_OPTIONS]\n```\n\n### Global Options\n\n| Option | Short | Description | Default |\n|--------|-------|-------------|---------|\n| `--config <FILE>` | `-C` | Config file path | `.needle.yaml` |\n| `--help` | `-h` | Show help | - |\n| `--version` | `-V` | Show version | - |\n\n### Run Command Options\n\n| Option | Description | Example |\n|--------|-------------|---------|\n| `-w, --workspace <PATH>` | Workspace directory | `-w /home/coding/ARMOR` |\n| `-c, --concurrency <NUM>` | Number of concurrent workers | `-c 1` |\n| `--strands <LIST>` | Specific strands to run | `--strands pluck,mend` |\n| `--one-shot` | Exit after one bead | - |\n\n## Pluck Strand Tracing Instrumentation\n\n### Tracing Span Fields\n\nThe Pluck strand uses `tracing::instrument` with the following fields:\n\n```rust\n#[tracing::instrument(\n    name = \"strand.pluck\",\n    skip(self, store),\n    fields(\n        strand = \"pluck\",\n        exclude_labels = ?self.exclude_labels,\n        split_threshold = self.split_after_failures,\n    )\n)]\n```\n\n### Debug Event Sequence\n\n#### 1. **Evaluation Start**\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Pluck strand evaluation starting\n  exclude_labels=[\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"]\n  split_threshold=3\n```\n\n#### 2. **Bead Store Query**\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Querying bead store for ready candidates\n  filters=Filters { \n    assignee: None, \n    exclude_labels: [\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"] \n  }\n```\n\n#### 3. **Query Results**\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Bead store returned N candidates\n  count=5\n```\n\n#### 4. **Label Filtering**\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Label filtering excluded N beads\n  excluded_count=2\n  remaining=3\n  excluded_labels=[\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"]\n```\n\n#### 5. **Individual Bead Exclusions**\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Excluded bead due to labels\n  bead_id=bf-1234\n  labels=[\"deferred\", \"high-priority\"]\n  excluded_reasons=[\"label:deferred\"]\n```\n\n#### 6. **Status/Assignee Filtering**\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Status/assignee filtering removed N beads\n  filtered_count=1\n  remaining=2\n```\n\n#### 7. **Candidate Sorting**\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Sorting N candidates by (priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)\n  total=5\n  first_bead_id=bf-abcd\n  first_priority=1\n  first_created_at=2026-07-09T12:00:00Z\n```\n\n#### 8. **Split Trigger Check**\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Checking split trigger for first candidate\n  bead_id=bf-fail\n  failure_count=4\n  threshold=3\n  split_triggered=true\n```\n\n#### 9. **Split Decision**\n```\nINFO needle::strand::pluck: Split threshold reached, returning Split instruction\n  bead_id=bf-fail\n  failure_count=4\n  threshold=3\n```\n\n#### 10. **Final Result**\n```\nINFO needle::strand::pluck: Returning N candidates for processing\n  count=2\n  candidates=[\"bf-abcd\", \"bf-1234\"]\n```\n\n### Error Events\n\n```\nERROR needle::strand::pluck: Bead store query failed\n  error=bf list failed\n```\n\n## Practical Execution Examples\n\n### Example 1: Standard Debug Session\n```bash\n#!/bin/bash\nWORKSPACE=\"/home/coding/ARMOR\"\nLOG_FILE=\"logs/pluck-debug/pluck-debug-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log\"\n\nmkdir -p logs/pluck-debug\n\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug \\\nNEEDLE run -w \"$WORKSPACE\" -c 1 2>&1 | tee \"$LOG_FILE\"\n\n# Analysis\ngrep \"Pluck strand evaluation starting\" \"$LOG_FILE\"\ngrep \"result=BeadFound\" \"$LOG_FILE\"\ngrep \"result=NoWork\" \"$LOG_FILE\"\n```\n\n### Example 2: Comprehensive Multi-Module filtering)\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug/[.]*pluck/ \\\nNEEDLE run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1\n```\n\n### Example 4: JSON Output for Analysis\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug \\\nNEEDLE run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1 2>&1 | jq -c 'select(.message | contains(\"pluck\"))'\n```\n\n## Log Analysis Commands\n\n### View All Pluck Events\n```bash\ngrep -i \"pluck\" pluck-debug.log\n```\n\n### Filter Specific Decisions\n```bash\n# Label filtering\ngrep -i \"filter\" pluck-debug.log\ngrep -i \"exclude\" pluck-debug.log\n\n# Candidate processing\ngrep -i \"candidate\" pluck-debug.log\n\n# Split decisions\ngrep -i \"split\" pluck-debug.log\n\n# Errors\ngrep -i \"error\" pluck-debug.log\n```\n\n### Count Event Types\n```bash\ngrep -c \"Pluck strand evaluation starting\" pluck-debug.log\ngrep -c \"result=BeadFound\" pluck-debug.log\ngrep -c \"result=NoWork\" pluck-debug.log\ngrep -c \"result=Split\" pluck-debug.log\ngrep -c \"Excluded bead due to labels\" pluck-debug.log\n```\n\n### Extract Specific Information\n```bash\n# Get all excluded bead IDs\ngrep \"Excluded bead due to labels\" pluck-debug.log | grep -oP 'bead_id=\\K[^,]+'\n\n# Get candidate counts\ngrep \"Bead store returned\" pluck-debug.log | grep -oP 'count=\\K\\d+'\n\n# View split threshold checks\ngrep \"Checking split trigger\" pluck-debug.log -A 1\n```\n\n## Additional Environment Variables\n\n### RUST_BACKTRACE\n```bash\n# Enable backtrace on errors\nRUST_BACKTRACE=1\n\n# Full backtrace (more detailed)\nRUST_BACKTRACE=full\n```\n\n### RUST_LOG_SPAN_EVENTS\n```bash\n# Show span enter/exit events\nRUST_LOG_SPAN_EVENTS=new,close\n```\n\n## Configuration File Integration\n\n### .needle.yaml Configuration\n\nThe Pluck strand behavior is controlled via `.needle.yaml`:\n\n```yaml\nstrands:\n  pluck:\n    exclude_labels: []  # Empty = use defaults (deferred, human, blocked)\n    split_after_failures: 0  # 0 = disabled, positive = enable auto-split\n```\n\n### Relationship to Debug Logging\n\nThe `.needle.yaml` file configures **behavior**, while `RUST_LOG` configures **visibility**. For comprehensive debugging:\n\n1. Set your desired behavior in `.needle.yaml`\n2. Set appropriate `RUST_LOG` level for visibility\n3. Run with command structure shown above\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### No Pluck Output Visible\n\n**Symptoms:** No pluck events in log despite setting RUST_LOG\n\n**Solutions:**\n```bash\n# Verify RUST_LOG is set\necho $RUST_LOG\n\n# Check if Pluck strand is active\ngrep \"worker booted\" pluck-debug.log | grep \"pluck\"\n\n# Ensure beads exist\nbr list --status=open\n```\n\n### Missing Expected Events\n\n**Common causes:**\n1. RUST_LOG syntax error\n2. Wrong module target\n3. No beads in queue\n4. Strand not loaded\n\n**Verification:**\n```bash\n# Test basic logging\nRUST_LOG=info needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1\n\n# Check strand loading\nRUST_LOG=needle::worker=debug needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1\n```\n\n### Performance Impact\n\nHigher debug levels (especially `trace`) can impact performance:\n\n| Level | Performance Impact | When to Use |\n|-------|-------------------|-------------|\n| `info` | Minimal | Production monitoring |\n| `debug` | Low | **Recommended for debugging** |\n| `trace` | Moderate | Deep troubleshooting only |\n| `trace` (global) | High | Short debugging sessions only |\n\n## Advanced Topics\n\n### Custom Log Formatting\n\nNEEDLE uses `tracing-subscriber` with JSON support:\n\n```bash\n# JSON output for parsing\nRUST_LOG_FORMAT=json RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug \\\nNEEDLE run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1\n```\n\n### OpenTelemetry Integration\n\nNEEDLE includes OTLP support (default feature):\n\n```bash\n# With OTLP endpoint (when configured)\nOTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=http://localhost:4317 \\\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug \\\nNEEDLE run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1\n```\n\n### Conditional Tracing\n\nThe Pluck source uses conditional `tracing::instrument`:\n\n```rust\n#[tracing::instrument(\n    name = \"strand.pluck\",\n    skip(self, store),\n    fields(\n        strand = \"pluck\",\n        exclude_labels = ?self.exclude_labels,\n        split_threshold = self.split_after_failures,\n    )\n)]\n```\n\n## Reference Documentation\n\n### Rust Tracing Documentation\n- [tracing crate](https://docs.rs/tracing/)\n- [tracing-subscriber](https://docs.rs/tracing-subscriber/)\n- [env_logger](https://docs.rs/env_logger/)\n\n### NEEDLE Documentation\n- Version: 0.2.11\n- Repository: `/home/coding/NEEDLE`\n- Pluck source: `src/strand/pluck.rs`\n\n## Summary\n\nThe complete Pluck debug command structure is:\n\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug \\\nNEEDLE run -w <workspace> -c <concurrency> 2>&1 | tee <output-file>\n```\n\n**Recommended for most debugging:**\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug,needle::strand=info,needle::worker=info \\\nNEEDLE run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1 2>&1 | tee pluck-debug-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log\n```\n\n**For comprehensive system debugging:**\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug \\\nRUST_BACKTRACE=1 \\\nNEEDLE run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1 2>&1 | tee pluck-comprehensive-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log\n```\n\n## Appendix: Complete Module Reference\n\n### NEEDLE Module Hierarchy\n\n```\nneedle\n├── strand\n│   ├── pluck      ← Primary bead selection\n│   ├── mend       → Bead completion\n│   ├── explore    → Discovery/research\n│   ├── weave      → Documentation\n│   ├── unravel    → Undo/revert\n│   ├── pulse      → Health checks\n│   ├── reflect    → Review/analysis\n│   ├── splice     → Code surgery\n│   └── knot       → Dependency management\n├── bead_store     → Storage backend\n├── worker         → Worker lifecycle\n├── dispatch       → Task distribution\n└── claim          → Bead claiming\n```\n\n### Log Level Severity\n\n```\nERROR  → Critical failures\nWARN   → Warning conditions\nINFO   → High-level operations (recommended for production)\nDEBUG  → Detailed decisions (recommended for debugging)\nTRACE  → Complete execution flow\n```\n\n---\n\n**Document Status:** ✅ Complete  \n**Verification:** ✅ Command syntax verified against NEEDLE 0.2.11 source code  \n**Ready for execution:**
